confirmation.detail: inbox envelope env_d76d239f-6734-449e-a4ec-1d01356e6fd0 read back as promoted

evidence: Our current workflow runs Narada proper from WSL while targeting Windows paths such as C:/Users/Andrey/Narada and ProgramData PC Site roots. In that case wsl_assisted is the ergonomic default. But a WSL runtime targeting /home or /var roots should be treated as WSL/Linux native, not as a Windows-assisted mutation.
proposal: Implement execution surface inference from executor runtime plus target authority/root. If executor_runtime=wsl and target_authority_locus is windows_user or windows_pc, infer execution_surface=wsl_assisted. If executor_runtime=wsl and target_authority_locus is wsl or linux_user/linux_system, infer wsl_native or linux_user/linux_system as appropriate. If executor_runtime=windows, infer windows_native. Ambiguous cases should require an explicit --execution-surface flag.
payload.recommendation: Dry-run output should state inferred execution surface loudly before mutation, for example: Detected WSL executor targeting Windows user Site; defaulting execution_surface=wsl_assisted; target authority remains windows_user. This keeps the ergonomic default while preserving the authority/execution split.
payload.summary: When bootstrapping or upgrading Windows Sites, Narada should default to wsl_assisted if the executor is WSL and the target is a Windows user-locus or PC-locus Site. It should not default from WSL detection alone, because WSL may also host WSL/Linux-locus Sites that own Linux-side state.
payload.title: WSL-assisted execution should default only from runtime plus target locus

promotion.note: recorded_pending_crossing: executable promotion for target kind 'site_config_change' is not implemented yet.
